name: Venice Beach Skate Park
description: Iconic oceanfront skate park featuring a historic bowl, street course, and stunning Pacific Ocean views. A legendary destination for street and bowl skating with a vibrant community.

name: House Park
description: Massive public skate park with diverse features including pools, bowls, street course, and flow sections. One of the largest skateboarding facilities in North America.

name: Burnside Skate Park
description: Historic under-bridge skate park built by skaters for skaters. Features a distinctive bowl design with unique urban character and strong community support.

name: Cheesman Park Skate Area
description: Modern urban skate park with street course, small bowls, and technical features. Located near downtown Denver with easy accessibility and free admission.

name: FDR Park Skate Plaza
description: Premier public skate park with professional-grade street course, mellow bowl, and flow sections. Excellent for all skill levels with quality terrain and regular events.

name: Tompkins Square Skate Park
description: Historic East Village skate spot in the heart of Manhattan. Compact but beloved park with street features and strong skating community presence.

name: Desert Skate Park
description: Modern public skate park featuring street course, bowl, and flow sections. Clean facilities with dedicated beginner areas and shaded rest zones.

name: Magnuson Park Skate Area
description: Large waterfront skate park with multiple bowls, street course, and beautiful views of Lake Washington. Free public facility with excellent features.

name: Burnham Skate Park
description: Urban lakefront skate park in Burnham Park along Lake Michigan. Features quality street course, bowl, and modern facilities with lake views.

name: Wilmington Skate Park
description: Comprehensive public skate park with street course, transition features, and beginner zones. Excellent community park serving all skill levels with free admission.
